design_philosophy.core_loop: Design and manage a railway network, balance between passenger transport profitability and cargo delivery for city growth, construct supplementary revenue buildings
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Profitability vs. City Development - passenger services generate immediate revenue but building materials enable long-term city growth and expansion
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Build and control a thriving railroad empire that shapes urban development and economic prosperity
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: No rival companies - the player operates as sole railroad monopoly in the city. Game is deliberately open-ended with multiple valid playstyles: pure profit optimization, city development as goal, or hybrid approach. Emphasizes emergent sandbox play over scripted objectives

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Fully player-controlled - no fixed difficulty settings mentioned. Players can set their own goals (profit targets, city size, specific development patterns). Computer generates building responses but does not compete

progression.upgrade_system: City evolves organically based on material delivery - houses develop into shops and commercial buildings as population and resources increase. Player can construct premium buildings (ski resorts, hotels) for additional revenue streams
progression.unlock_mechanism: City infrastructure unlocks through delivery of building materials to zones; player structures unlock based on conditions like population density and existing infrastructure
replayability_hooks: Construction Set editor allows creating custom landscapes from scratch
Multiple win conditions possible (profit maximization, city size, specific development patterns)
Sandbox nature supports indefinite play and experimentation

world_building_techniques: Organic urban growth responding to player infrastructure decisions
Visual progression from rural houses to developed commercial districts

environmental_storytelling: City development patterns reflect player's strategic choices
Visual evolution from sparse buildings to dense urban centers
Building type distribution indicates economic health and player priorities

sources_used: wikipedia - A-Train series article (A-Train III section)
